primary_finding.statement: Dominant IC peak shrinks faster than proportional capacity loss: slope of A(n)/A₀ vs SOH(n) ≈ 1.9 across all cells (1.0 = proportional). This is a genuine LAM-adjacent signal: for each 1% of total SOH lost, the phase-transition peak loses ~1.9% of its initial height.

circularity_finding.statement: A(n)/A₀ tracks SOH(n) with r ≈ 0.99 (R² ≈ 0.97–0.99) for all cells. This means the raw peak-area fraction ΔA_rel is algebraically equivalent to ΔSOH: ΔA_rel ≡ ΔSOH up to a linear rescaling. Any regression of ΔSOH on ΔA_rel achieves R² ≈ 0.99 by circularity, NOT by discovering a physical LLI/LAM decomposition.
circularity_finding.implication: A reported 'LAM % of total fade' derived from this regression would be a statistical artifact. The slope deviation from 1.0 (the ~1.9× factor) is the only information in A(n) that is NOT already present in SOH(n).

peak_stability_note: Two distinct claims about K (peak count) must not be conflated: (1) TOTAL K COUNT varies 3-12 across cells at prom=0.015; extra peaks are noise structures >150 mV from the dominant peak (median separation >380 mV). (2) DOMINANT PEAK TRACKING: 168/168 cycles tracked for B0005/B0007 with 0 jumps >50 mV; 132/132 for B0018 with 0 jumps >50 mV and 2 oscillations of ±30 mV (investigated, confirmed noise within adjacent-peak cluster, not tracker jumps to a different peak). The headline slope_m and Δμ numbers depend on dominant-peak tracking quality, not total K count.

per_cell_status.B0005: quantitative — dominant peak stable (0 jumps >50 mV in 168 cycles; K=3-10 total, extra peaks are noise >150 mV from dominant)
per_cell_status.B0006: qualitative only — K unstable (2-11) coupled with 2× larger drift; peak identity ambiguous across lifetime
per_cell_status.B0007: single-factor — dominant peak stable (0 jumps >50 mV in 168 cycles; K=2-10 total); LLI and LAM structurally collinear (r=−0.927), cause is co-monotonicity NOT identity swap (verified cycle-by-cycle)
per_cell_status.B0018: quantitative — dominant peak stable (0 jumps >50 mV in 132 cycles; 2 oscillations ±30 mV at cycles 73-74 and 126-127 investigated, confirmed as noise within 30 mV of peak, not tracking errors; K=4-12 total)

B0005.K_peak_count_note: Total K varies 3-10 (prom=0.015). Extra peaks beyond the dominant are noise structures in the 2.7-3.4 V region (prominence ≤0.5), median >380 mV from dominant peak at ~3.49 V (prominence ~5.0). Dominant peak tracked 168/168 cycles, 0 jumps >25 mV. K drops to 3-5 in late life (cycles 111+) as low-voltage IC structures collapse — IC simplification, not tracking failure.

LLI_peak_drift.interpretation: stoichiometric window narrowing consistent with LLI (SEI-driven)

LAM_slope_ratio.interpretation: peak shrinks FASTER than capacity → genuine LAM-adjacent signal
LAM_slope_ratio.caveat: quantitative; circularity means raw A% ≡ SOH%; slope deviation is the genuine signal

CL_resistance.source: EIS Re/Rct stored directly in NASA .mat — no dV/I approximation
CL_resistance.note_first_19: first 19 discharges lack preceding EIS (EIS starts at cycle index 40)

B0006.K_peak_count_note: Total K varies 2-11 (prom=0.015). Unlike B0005/B0007/B0018, B0006 shows genuine peak-identity ambiguity: new low-voltage peaks emerge and merge over its lifetime (total drift 171 mV vs 52-73 mV for other cells). The dominant-peak identity shifts over the cell's lifetime, making slope_m unreliable. QUALITATIVE ONLY.

LLI_peak_drift.interpretation: stoichiometric window narrowing consistent with LLI (SEI-driven)

LAM_slope_ratio.interpretation: peak shrinks FASTER than capacity → genuine LAM-adjacent signal

CL_resistance.source: EIS Re/Rct stored directly in NASA .mat — no dV/I approximation
CL_resistance.note_first_19: first 19 discharges lack preceding EIS (EIS starts at cycle index 40)

B0007.K_peak_count_note: Total K varies 2-10 (prom=0.015). Extra peaks are noise structures >150 mV from dominant, with 6.5% of cycles (11/168) at high-K — extra peaks 89% of the time >150 mV from dominant (median separation 444 mV). Dominant peak tracked 168/168 cycles, 0 jumps >50 mV, 0 jumps >25 mV. Collinearity (r=−0.927) is structural co-monotonicity, not an artifact of K instability.

LLI_peak_drift.interpretation: stoichiometric window narrowing consistent with LLI (SEI-driven)

LAM_slope_ratio.interpretation: peak shrinks FASTER than capacity → genuine LAM-adjacent signal
LAM_slope_ratio.caveat: slope computable but LLI/LAM not separable — structural co-monotonicity (r=−0.927)

CL_resistance.source: EIS Re/Rct stored directly in NASA .mat — no dV/I approximation
CL_resistance.note_first_19: first 19 discharges lack preceding EIS (EIS starts at cycle index 40)

B0018.K_peak_count_note: Total K varies 4-12 (prom=0.015). Extra peaks at high-K cycles (4/132, 3%) are noise structures >150 mV from dominant (median separation 389 mV); dominant peak at those cycles matches surrounding-cycle trajectory within ≤14 mV. Dominant peak tracked 132/132 cycles, 0 jumps >50 mV. Two oscillations of ±30 mV at cycle windows 72-77 and 123-129 were investigated: tracker bounces among closely-spaced peaks all within ±30 mV of the true 3.41-3.47 V dominant peak — noise in peak position estimate, not a jump to a different peak. Status confirmed: quantitative.

LLI_peak_drift.interpretation: stoichiometric window narrowing consistent with LLI (SEI-driven)

LAM_slope_ratio.interpretation: peak shrinks FASTER than capacity → genuine LAM-adjacent signal
LAM_slope_ratio.caveat: quantitative; circularity means raw A% ≡ SOH%; slope deviation is the genuine signal

CL_resistance.B0018_Rct_note: B0018 first 3 EIS cycles show Rct=95–102 mΩ (anomalous high; likely temperature/conditioning artifact). Steady-state Rct≈84–92 mΩ. Robust delta (first-10%% vs last-10%% of matched cycles) used throughout.
CL_resistance.source: EIS Re/Rct stored directly in NASA .mat — no dV/I approximation

references: Dubarry M, Anseán D. 'Best practices for incremental capacity analysis.' Front. Energy Res. 10:1023555 (2022). doi:10.3389/fenrg.2022.1023555
Sulzer V, et al. 'The challenge and opportunity of battery lifetime prediction from field data.' Joule 5(8):1934-1955 (2021). doi:10.1016/j.joule.2021.06.005
Saha B, Goebel K. NASA Ames Prognostics Data Repository (2007).
honest_limitations: No half-cell reference data: true LLI/LAM separation (Dubarry & Anseán 2022) requires fitting U_pos(x)−U_neg(y); without it all mode attributions are phenomenological.
NASA B0005–B0018 are LCO/NMC 18650 cells with broad IC peaks (FWHM ≈ 80–150 mV). Peak shift signals of 30–85 mV are physically real but close to IC noise floor.
Regression-based LLI/LAM quantification is circular (see circularity_finding above). Slope ratio is the correct quantitative metric.

B0006 K-instability is genuine peak-identity ambiguity (total drift 171 mV, new peaks emerge and merge) — distinct from B0005/B0007/B0018 where K instability is harmless noise-peak variation far from the dominant peak.

Total K count (3-12 at prom=0.015) is NOT the same as dominant-peak tracking stability. The headline slope_m and Δμ numbers are grounded in dominant-peak tracking, which has 0 jumps >50 mV for all cells. A pre-run diagnostic at only 3 sampled cycles (1, mid, final) at prom=0.02 showed K=5 stable — this was later found to be a spot-check artifact; the full-run K distribution was investigated cycle-by-cycle and the dominant peak tracking was confirmed robust.

_provenance.B0006_exclusion_commit: 0dbbe37 (2026-07-05 22:58:00 +0530) — Module 3 initial commit; B0006 marked qualitative-only before cross-module question was posed
_provenance.cross_module_question_posed: after c224fe2; B0006 exclusion pre-dates this analysis
_provenance.note: Verify via: git show 0dbbe37 --follow -p -- data/nasa_ica_report.json | grep -A4 per_cell_status

_statistical_power.statement: No result from Check A can be statistically significant (n=3; min achievable p=1/6=0.167). Check B is underpowered (n=4). This is a plausibility check and coherence audit — not a validated correlation.
_statistical_power.D_per_cycle_note: All four cells share identical protocol (1C CC, DoD≈100%, T≈31-33°C). D_per_cycle≈constant across cells; cross-cell variation in β captures mechanical susceptibility, not stress dose.

anchor_check.purpose: Ground-truth: β should rank-order with actual SOH fade
anchor_check.inversion: B0005 (β-rank 2, fade-rank 3) vs B0018 (β-rank 3, fade-rank 2) — actual fade difference 0.9% (28.6% vs 27.7%), within measurement noise. B0006 and B0007 agree perfectly.
anchor_check.assessment: β is a reasonable proxy for degradation magnitude at this n; single inversion is noise between two nearly-identical fade values.

check_A_LAM_vs_stress.pre_registered_interpretation: Suggests the two modules are measuring different degradation axes. Stress-fatigue model may be capturing a signal (faster capacity drop early) that is distinct from phase-transition peak loss. Honest conclusion: no evidence for stress→LAM coupling in these cells; mechanisms appear independent.
check_A_LAM_vs_stress.critical_qualifier: slope_m range across the 3 cells is 0.049 (1.854–1.903), which is ~2.6% of the baseline value (~1.9). This is within IC fitting noise. The 'perfect disagreement' rs=-1.0 is a rank ordering of near-identical values; it does not represent a meaningful anti-correlation. The pre-registered category is PERFECT DISAGREEMENT, and the conclusion stands, but a reader should understand the signal amplitude is negligible — all three cells show slope_m≈1.9 ± noise.

check_B_CL_vs_stress.metric: ΔRe+ΔRct vs β (and Re-only, Rct-only separately)
key_discordances: B0007: rank_β=1 (lowest, most resistant to stress-fatigue) but rank_ΔR=4 (highest resistance rise, 54.8mΩ). 3-rank swap. B0007's impedance growth appears driven by a mechanism independent of stress-fatigue susceptibility.

B0018: rank_β=3 (second-highest stress susceptibility) but rank_ΔR=1 (essentially flat, ΔR=3.5mΩ). B0018's capacity loss from stress is not accompanied by resistance growth — possibly because its fade is SEI-like (γ<1=0.887, decelerating) rather than particle-crack driven.
ΔRe-only and ΔRct-only give identical rs=-0.40: the discordance is not specific to one resistance component — B0007 is anomalously high in both Re and Rct relative to its β.
check_B_CL_vs_stress.pre_registered_interpretation: CL and stress-fatigue proceed with no clear coupling. B0007's anomalously high resistance despite low β suggests its impedance growth is driven by a mechanism other than mechanical stress (possibly SEI growth rate or batch-specific chemistry). B0018's near-zero ΔR despite moderate β is consistent with its γ<1 (decelerating fade, possibly SEI-dominated) rather than particle-cracking profile.
check_B_CL_vs_stress.B0007_outlier_note: B0007 was already flagged in Module 3 as having ΔRe+ΔRct=54.8mΩ, 1.3-15× larger than other cells. Its removal would change rs for n=3 (B0005, B0006, B0018) but this was not pre-registered and is not computed here.
check_B_CL_vs_stress.B0018_Rct_caveat: B0018 ΔRct=-0.2mΩ uses robust delta (first 10% vs last 10% of matched EIS cycles) to correct for conditioning artifact in first 3 EIS cycles (Rct=95-102mΩ vs steady-state ~84mΩ). The robust delta is itself valid but the near-zero result means B0018's Rct trajectory was flat across its 132 discharge lifetime.

overall_conclusion.statement: No evidence for stress-fatigue → LAM or stress-fatigue → CL coupling in these 4 cells at n=3/4. Both checks fall into their respective 'no coupling' pre-registered categories (PERFECT DISAGREEMENT and WEAK/MIXED). The two modules appear to be capturing independent degradation axes in this dataset.
overall_conclusion.physical_interpretation: All cells share identical protocol (1C, 100% DoD), so β variation reflects cell-to-cell manufacturing susceptibility, not controlled stress amplitude. In this regime, β co-varies with total capacity fade (rs=0.8 anchor) but not with IC peak degradation rate (slope_m) or resistance rise (ΔR). This is consistent with Sulzer 2021's finding that field/lab fade at this depth is dominated by SEI chemistry rather than mechanical particle cracking — both Module 2's β and Module 3's slope_m may be measuring facets of the same SEI-driven process, manifesting differently in the two measurement domains.
what_would_be_needed_to_test_the_stress_: Controlled DoD sweep (e.g., 25%/50%/75%/100% DoD, same cells or matched chemistry) — stress amplitude variation is required, not just cell-to-cell susceptibility variation at fixed DoD
n≥10 cells to have statistical power to resolve correlations
Half-cell reference to confirm LAM attribution in slope_m is genuinely active-material structural loss, not stoichiometric
overall_conclusion.n3_power_restatement: This cross-module synthesis is a coherence audit, not a validated result. The pre-registered interpretations are applied, but no causal claim about stress driving LAM or CL is supported or refuted by these 4 cells.
